accepted_claims: HeteroKV is an Approximate Long-Context Cache, not a lossless full-KV replica.
FullKV 128K under a 22 GiB PyTorch cap OOMs with both eager attention and SDPA, while HeteroKV 128K runs under the same cap.
HeteroKV active HBM KV remains bounded in 128K NIAH runs: max_reserved is consistently about 20.65 GiB, with nvidia-smi process memory about 21.0-21.5 GiB.
Cue-focus alpha0.65 with Method-D token-level dot-product retrieval passed 128K required-depth NIAH on seeds 4242 and 6004: 16/16.
Source-aware Method-D reuse TTL plus token_window=64 passed 128K required-depth NIAH on seeds 4242, 6004, and 7777: 20/20, while reducing decode latency to roughly 520-563 ms/token.
A real 8K WikiText-2 PPL suffix run passed the <=5% degradation target: FullKV PPL 7.2859 vs HeteroKV PPL 7.5443, +3.55%.
A real 10K WikiText-2 PPL suffix run also passed: FullKV PPL 4.9011 vs HeteroKV PPL 4.9046, +0.07%, with process peak about 22.6 GiB.

Path A Triton retrieval scoring is approved and partially validated at the mechanism level: Stage1 passes, warm microbench matched PyTorch top-k after the batched reducer fix, and a 32K real-model NIAH smoke passed with triton_int4_batch backend logs.

The GPU monitor harness was corrected: long Method-D runs must redirect stdout/stderr to a file or actively drain pipes, otherwise mechanism logs can fill an unconsumed pipe and falsely stall the child process.
Source-cue answer-span physical retrieval is implemented as an opt-in mechanism and passed Stage1 CPU/GPU tests plus 32K and 128K real-model NIAH probes under the 22 GiB cap.
Cue-context focus-only retrieval with context=3 passed 128K required-depth NIAH on seeds 4242, 6004, and 7777: 20/20, with process peaks about 21.1 GiB and max_reserved about 20.65 GiB.
Under context=3, retrieved K/V cache reuse was retested and passed 128K required-depth NIAH on seeds 4242, 6004, and 7777: 20/20, with decode around 551-680 ms/step on the shared GPU2 runs.
The 10K WikiText-2 PPL suffix test was refreshed with retrieve_focus_only=True and retrieve_focus_context_tokens=3: FullKV PPL 4.9011 vs HeteroKV PPL 4.9046, +0.07%.
blocked_claims: Workflow3 / paper-writing is not ready: latency remains far above a <=2x FullKV SDPA decode baseline.
Optional 0% NIAH boundary still fails under the main configuration and under the source-before-min diagnostic branch; do not claim full boundary robustness.
The PPL evidence is real and now includes 8K and 10K suffix evaluations, but it is still not a broad 128K language-modeling suite.
A100-under-22GiB evidence supports a 4090-like memory-envelope survival claim, but not true 4090 latency.
Path A is not an accepted main path: 128K required-depth accuracy was 2/4 even after FP16 dequant rounding in the Triton kernel.

Retrieved K/V cache reuse is not accepted as a default optimization: it improved a 32K smoke runtime but 128K required-depth accuracy was 2/4, so it is now behind method_d_reuse_kv_cache and defaults off.
A new seed6004 one-trial required-depth pairing exposed a main-path robustness gap: PyTorch scoring with selected-key reuse found the needle chunk but still scored only 2/4.
Path A is retrieval scoring only; fused V weighting / fused attention output remains unimplemented and unclaimed.
Latency remains much slower than the relaxed FullKV SDPA reference and was measured under shared-GPU contention, so Workflow3 is still blocked.

latest_success.summary: Context=3 plus retrieved K/V cache reuse completed the third-seed 128K required-depth check, giving 20/20 across seeds 4242, 6004, and 7777 with stable memory.

latest_failure.summary: Pure answer-span physical retrieval with context=0 scored 7/8 on seed6004 broader required-depth testing, motivating cue-context retrieval.

active_hypothesis: The strongest current candidate is source-aware cue focus plus token-level Method-D retrieval, selected-key reuse TTL6, token_window64, source-cue focus-only physical retrieval, retrieve_focus_context_tokens=3, and opt-in retrieved K/V cache reuse. It fixes the answer-generation failure class while improving decode latency, but the <=2x latency claim is still blocked.

next_experiment.name: fair_latency_or_next_latency_optimization
next_experiment.purpose: Obtain a fairer same-GPU latency comparison for context=3 + K/V cache; if it remains far above target, continue with structural latency optimizations rather than Workflow3.
next_experiment.safety: Continue using no-pipe monitoring for long GPU runs; keep the 30 GiB own-process fuse; avoid GPU3 while the VideoMME job remains active.

last_local_process_update: Workflow2 validated context=3 + retrieved K/V cache across three seeds, refreshed the 10K PPL check, and kept Workflow3 blocked because latency is still the unresolved acceptance item.
